data.title: VinFast starts construction on a new plant in Vietnam to boost capacity
data.description:
Vietnamese electric car manufacturer VinFast Auto Ltd. (NASDAQ:VFS) broke ground on the construction of its second plant in Vietnam. The company aims to double its production capacity in the country to 600,000 vehicles from 300,000 vehicles.

The new $290 million factory in the Ha Tinh province will mainly produce the VF 3 and VF 5 models for both domestic sales and export after an eight-month construction period, according to VinFast (VFS). The capacity increase by the electric vehicle maker was noted to be due to the increasing demand for small and medium-sized models. "Demand in international markets is also growing rapidly, so the construction of an additional electric car factory in Ha Tinh will create a solid foundation for an important and explosive development phase of VinFast in the coming time," stated Nguyen Viet Quang, CEO of VinFast (VFS) parent company Vingroup.

During Q3, VinFast (VFS) delivered 21,912 vehicles to mark a 115% year-over-year increase. Revenue reached $511.6 million, which was up 49.3% from the same quarter a year ago. Notably, VinFast (VFS) became the top-selling automotive brand in Vietnam for September, October, and for the first ten months of 2024, while continuing to make strides in its global expansion.

Shares of VinFast (VFS) are up 33% over the last six weeks.

data.title: Lattice Semiconductor authorizes additional $100 million stock buyback program
data.description:
• Lattice Semiconductor (NASDAQ:LSCC) said its board authorized the company to buyback up to an additional $100 million of its outstanding common stock through the end of December 2025.
  • The firm has repurchased ~6 million shares since the fourth quarter of 2020.
  • The company said the 2025 buyback is effective immediately.

data.title: Brera Holdings invests in Juve Stabia
data.description:
• Brera Holdings (NASDAQ:BREA) has signed a binding term sheet to make a strategic investment in SS Juve Stabia SpA, a historic Italian Serie B football club celebrating 117 years of tradition, it said on Monday.
  • Known as 'The Second Team of Naples,' Juve Stabia is a key fixture in Italy’s third-largest metropolitan area.
  • “After a thorough analysis and negotiations with leading Serie B clubs, Brera Holdings is extremely proud to invest in Juve Stabia, an important club with an ideal partner in President Andrea Langella,” said Brera Executive Chairman Daniel McClory, Founder and majority shareholder.
  • BREA +5.59% premarket to $0.65.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Thames Water appoints AECOM to new £400 million professional services framework
data.description:
• AECOM (NYSE:ACM) said it was appointed by Thames Water to a new professional services framework in the UK.
  • The new £400 million framework, FA1980, covers asset, capital and engineering professional service.
  • The framework will help the utility to improve water and wastewater services, upgrade infrastructure, prevent leakages and pollution, and enhance the environment.

data.title: Dow unveils partnership with Macquarie, sells Gulf Coast asset stake in $2.4B deal
data.description:
Dow (NYSE:DOW) +5.1% pre-market Monday after saying it agreed to sell a 40% equity stake in some U.S. Gulf Coast infrastructure assets, with an option to increase its equity stake to 49% within six months of closing, to Macquarie Asset Management.

Dow (DOW) expects to receive ~$2.4B in initial cash proceeds based on the initial transaction, with potential to receive up to ~$3B in total.

The companies will launch a new partnership, Diamond Infrastructure Solutions, which will aim to "leverage Dow's operational excellence and Macquarie Asset Management's infrastructure and energy expertise to generate growth opportunities by opening access to Dow's U.S. Gulf Coast sites to third party customers."

Diamond will be comprised of certain non-product producing assets - power and steam production, pipelines, environmental operations and general site infrastructure - located at five of Dow's (DOW) manufacturing sites on the U.S. Gulf Coast: Freeport, Texas City, and Seadrift in Texas, as well as Plaquemine and St. Charles in Louisiana.

data.title: VirnetX names John Jamison as CTO
data.description:
• VirnetX (NYSE:VHC) said it has appointed John Jamison as the new chief technical officer.
  • The former CTO, Victor Larson, will take on a new role as a strategic advisor at VirnetX.

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher launches $8.5B stock offering
data.description:
Arthur J. Gallagher (NYSE:AJG) has launched an underwritten public offering of $8.5B of its common stock.

The company also intends to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase an additional $1.275B of shares of its common stock.

Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC is acting as lead active book-running manager and BofA Securities, Inc. is acting as active book-running manager for the offering.

Gallagher (AJG) plans to use the net proceeds of this offering to fund a portion of the cash consideration payable in connection with its proposed acquisition of Dolphin TopCo, the holding company of AssuredPartners.

In addition to the net proceeds from this offering, Gallagher expects to use available cash and available borrowings under its revolving credit facility or other borrowings to fund the purchase price for the transaction.

The offering is not conditioned on the closing of the transaction.

AJG shares fell 1.25% premarket on Monday.

data.title: EU and Mercosur finalize free trade deal, but roadblocks remain
data.description:
The European Union and four members of the South American trade bloc Mercosur - Brazil, Argentina, Paraguay and Uruguay - finalized a free trade agreement after 25 years of negotiations, although it still needs to be approved by all member nations.

The deal aims to remove tariffs on EU exports to Mercosur like cars, machinery and consumer products. It will diversify supply chains, save EU businesses €4B worth of duties per year, and includes environmental commitments.

"This is a win-win agreement, which will bring meaningful benefits to consumers and businesses, on both sides," said Ursula von der Leyen, president, European Commission. EU exports to Mercosur were €56B in goods in 2023 and €28B in services in 2022.

However, the agreement can still collapse, given opposition from EU members like France and Poland. Opponents are concerned that the flow of cheaper Mercosur imports will threaten EU sectors and won't meet the EU's strict food safety, environment and animal welfare standards.

But proponents like Spain and Germany said the deal would remove trade barriers, and Mercosur would become a source of critical minerals like lithium.

Farmers in Mercosur countries claim that the environmental commitments in the agreement are masked protectionism efforts by the EU.

An EU-Mercosur trade deal would be "welcome amid a global climate engulfed by a new era of protectionism… however, the likelihood of success remains slim," ING Economic and Financial Analysis said.

More on EU-Mercosur deal

  • EU, South American trade bloc reach a giant trade deal after 25 years of talks
  • EU And Mercosur Seal Agreement Following Decades-Long Negotiations

data.title: Las Vegas Strip casinos are having a tough time matching 2023 gaming revenue levels
data.description:
Analysts think Las Vegas Strip casinos could see the recent soft gaming revenue trends continue in November and December. Casinos stocks have stumbled compared to the broad market over the last few months, partially due to GGR marks from Las Vegas that have come in below last year’s level to temper investor confidence.

Las Vegas Strip casinos reported a 3.1% decline in gaming revenue in October to $714.4 million, according to data from the Nevada Gaming Control Board. Table game revenue was soft during the month, with baccarat revenue down 23% year-over-year and roulette revenue off 36%. Sportsbooks on the Strip saw revenue decline 50% during the month on a narrow hold percentage of just 3%.

Las Vegas Strip casinos have now recorded four straight months of declining year-over-year GGR.

On the positive side, Deutsche Bank Carlo Santarelli noted that no one is in a panic over the Strip decline, which he attributed mainly to a drop in baccarat hold and tough comparisons to the second half of 2023. "Looking ahead, the Strip faces tough comparisons in both November and December, months in which baccarat hold in 2023 was 19% and 22%, respectively," warned Santarelli. "We expect year-over-year gaming-revenue declines in both months and we believe hold around F1 was as expected — less favorable than it was in 2023," he added.

UBS analyst Robin Farley said the most of the Q4 results for the Strip operators will likely be determined by hold during F-1 and the year-end holidays. "In our recent meetings with WYNN and MGM both companies reminded investors that they are comping favorable hold in the prior year and other tough comps in Q4 (to be followed by tough Super Bowl comps in Q1.)," highlighted Farley.

Meanwhile, Truist Securities analyst Barry Jonas showed more caution and pointed to soft visitation data for Las Vegas in general and thinks the F1 room rate comparison to a year ago will be difficult to match when the November numbers roll in.

Other analysts have made more favorable comments, and think a pullback in consumer discretionary spending just ahead of the U.S. election could reverse and provide a notable tailwind into 2025. On a historical basis, the Strip numbers are still relatively strong, according to J.P. Morgan analyst Joseph Greff. He highlighted that Strip gaming revenue increased 29%, locals gaming revenue increased 27%, and statewide gaming increased 26%.

Sector watch: Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR), MGM Resorts (NYSE:MGM), Boyd Gaming (NYSE:BYD), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Full House Resorts (FLL), Bally's Corporation (BALY), and Red Rock Resorts (NASDAQ:RRR).

data.title: Healthcare Realty Trust names Austen Helfrich as permanent CFO
data.description:
• Healthcare Realty Trust (NYSE:HR) has formally appointed Austen Helfrich as the company’s permanent executive vice president and chief financial officer, effective immediately.
  • Helfrich has been serving as interim CFO since Oct. 1, 2024.
